docs.title: Distill
docs.text:
Turn your daily reading into a synthesized digest — powered by Claude.

Distill is a personal content pipeline that ingests RSS feeds, shared links, browser history, and social media — then uses Claude to synthesize a daily reading digest. It runs locally on your Mac, serves a web dashboard, and optionally publishes to Ghost, social media, or Obsidian.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
1. Intake — Pulls content from 90+ RSS feeds (built-in), plus any sources you add
  2. Enrich — Fetches full article text, auto-tags, extracts entities
  3. Synthesize — Claude reads everything and writes a concise daily digest
  4. Publish — Outputs to Obsidian markdown, Ghost CMS, or social platforms

docs.title: Minimum to get started
docs.text:
You need two things:

  • Python 3.11+ with uv
  • An Anthropic API key (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
# Clone and install
git clone https://github.com/nikspatel007/distill.git
cd distill
uv sync

# Set your API key
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Run your first digest
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --use-defaults

That's it. Distill ships with 90+ curated RSS feeds. Your first digest appears in ./insights/intake/.

docs.title: What's included
docs.text: Feature What you get Daily reading digest LLM-synthesized summary of your feeds Web dashboard Browse digests, manage shares, read articles Phone sharing Share URLs from your phone via iOS Shortcut Journal Auto-generated dev journal from coding sessions Blog Weekly/thematic blog posts from journal entries Social publishing Push content to Twitter, LinkedIn, Reddit via Postiz

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Quickstart — Full setup in 5 minutes
  • Configuration — Customize feeds, models, and outputs
  • Daily Automation — Set up a daily cron
  • Web Dashboard — Browse your digests in a browser
  • Share from Phone — iOS Shortcut + Tailscale setup

docs.title: Mobile Access (PWA)
docs.text: Distill can be installed as a Progressive Web App on your phone for a native app-like experience.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Distill dashboard running on your Mac
  • Tailscale installed on both Mac and phone (free for personal use)
  • Both devices on the same Tailnet

docs.title: Step 1: Install Tailscale
docs.text:
MaciPhone 
brew install tailscale
# Or download from https://tailscale.com/download/mac
tailscale up

Download Tailscale from the App Store and sign in with the same account.

docs.title: Step 2: Find your Mac's Tailscale hostname
docs.text:
tailscale status

Look for your machine name — it'll be something like your-macbook.tail12345.ts.net or a 100.x.x.x IP address.

docs.title: Step 3: Start the dashboard
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights

The server listens on port 6107 by default.

docs.title: Step 4: Open on your phone
docs.text:
On your iPhone, open Safari and navigate to:

http://your-macbook.tail12345.ts.net:6107

Use the Tailscale IP if hostname doesn't resolve

http://100.x.x.x:6107

docs.title: Step 5: Add to Home Screen
docs.text:
1. Tap the Share button in Safari (square with arrow)
  2. Scroll down and tap Add to Home Screen
  3. Name it "Distill" and tap Add

The app now launches in standalone mode (no Safari chrome) with the Distill icon.

docs.title: Optional: HTTPS with Tailscale certs
docs.text:
For a more secure setup:

# On your Mac
tailscale cert your-macbook.tail12345.ts.net

# Start with TLS
uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights \
  --tls-cert your-macbook.tail12345.ts.net.crt \
  --tls-key your-macbook.tail12345.ts.net.key

Then access via https://your-macbook.tail12345.ts.net:6117 on your phone.

docs.title: Offline support
docs.text: The PWA includes a service worker that caches static assets. If your Mac is unreachable, you'll see a basic offline page. Content requires a live connection to the server.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Can't reach the server from phone?

  • Verify both devices show as connected in the Tailscale app
  • Try pinging your Mac from the phone: open Safari, go to http://100.x.x.x:6107
  • Make sure the Distill server is running (lsof -i :6107)

PWA not updating?

  • Open the PWA, pull down to refresh
  • If stale, clear the site data in Safari Settings > Advanced > Website Data

docs.title: Web Dashboard
docs.text: Distill includes a web dashboard for browsing digests, managing shared links, and reading extracted articles.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
The dashboard requires Bun (JavaScript runtime):

curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash

docs.title: Starting the dashboard
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights

This:

  1. Installs JS dependencies (bun install) if needed
  2. Builds the frontend (bun run build) if needed
  3. Starts the API server on http://localhost:6107

First start takes ~30 seconds

Subsequent starts are instant since the build is cached.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text: Page What it shows Home Pipeline status, recent activity, quick stats Reading Daily intake digests with full markdown rendering Graph Knowledge graph with three tabs (see below) Shares URLs shared from your phone — click to read extracted content Studio Blog post editor with AI chat and multi-platform publishing Settings Configuration, source management, sharing setup

docs.title: Knowledge Graph
docs.text:
The Graph page (/graph) has three tabs:

  • Briefing — Executive summary generated from your recent sessions. Shows focus areas with status (active/cooling/emerging), learning topics, risks, and recommendations.
  • Activity — Session timeline with stats, entity tag cloud, and file activity over a configurable time window (24h–30d).
  • Concepts — Interactive mind-map of ~25 concept nodes extracted from the briefing. Projects, topics, threads, risks, and entities are shown as labeled rectangles with color-coded edges. Click a node to see its detail and connections. Drag nodes to rearrange the layout.

Generate briefing data first

The Briefing and Concepts tabs require a briefing file. Generate one with: 

uv run python -m distill graph briefing --output ./insights

docs.title: Port
docs.text:
The default port is 6107. Override with:

uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights --port 8080

docs.title: HTTPS / TLS
docs.text:
For Tailscale or remote access, enable HTTPS:

# Generate certs with Tailscale
tailscale cert your-machine.ts.net

# Start with TLS
uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights \
  --tls-cert your-machine.ts.net.crt \
  --tls-key your-machine.ts.net.key

This binds HTTPS on port 6117 alongside HTTP on 6107.

docs.title: Development mode
docs.text:
For frontend development with hot reload:

# Terminal 1: API server
uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights --dev

# Terminal 2: Vite dev server (HMR)
cd web && bun run dev

The Vite dev server on port 6108 proxies API requests to 6107.

docs.title: Sharing from Phone
docs.text:
Share articles from any app on your phone directly into your next intake digest. The flow:

Copy URL → Run Shortcut (Back Tap) → Distill saves it → Next intake digest includes it

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. You copy a URL on your phone (from X, Safari, LinkedIn, etc.)
  2. An iOS Shortcut reads your clipboard and sends it to the Distill API
  3. The URL is saved to .distill-shares.json in your output directory
  4. When the next intake pipeline runs, it fetches the full article text and includes it with priority in the digest
  5. You can read the extracted content in the Shares page of the dashboard

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Distill dashboard running on your Mac
  • Tailscale connecting your phone and Mac (see PWA setup)
  • Your server's Tailscale address (e.g., 100.x.x.x or your-mac.ts.net)

docs.title: Step 1: Find your sharing URL
docs.text:
Your sharing endpoint is:

http://<your-tailscale-address>:6107/api/shares?url=

For example: http://100.64.0.1:6107/api/shares?url=

Store this in your config

Add your hostname to .distill.toml so the Settings page shows the correct URL:

[server]
hostname = "100.64.0.1"

docs.title: Step 2: Create the iOS Shortcut
docs.text:
1. Open the Shortcuts app on your iPhone
  2. Tap + to create a new shortcut
  3. Add these actions in order:

docs.title: Action 1: Get Clipboard
docs.text: • Search for "Get Clipboard" and add it

docs.title: Action 2: Get Contents of URL
docs.text:
• Search for "Get Contents of URL"
  • Set the URL to:
http://<your-tailscale-address>:6107/api/shares?url=
  • Tap on the URL field and append the Clipboard variable after ?url=
  • The final URL should look like: http://100.64.0.1:6107/api/shares?url=[Clipboard]

docs.title: Action 3: Show Notification
docs.text:
• Search for "Show Notification"

  • Set the message to: Saved to Distill

  • Name the shortcut "Send to Distill"

  • Tap Done

docs.title: Step 3: Set up Back Tap (optional, recommended)
docs.text:
Instead of opening the Shortcuts app each time, trigger it with a double-tap on the back of your phone:

  1. Go to Settings > Accessibility > Touch > Back Tap
  2. Choose Double Tap or Triple Tap
  3. Scroll down to Shortcuts and select "Send to Distill"

Now the workflow is:

  1. See an interesting article in any app
  2. Copy the URL (long-press the link, tap Copy)
  3. Double-tap the back of your phone
  4. See the "Saved to Distill" notification

docs.title: During intake pipeline
docs.text:
When distill intake or distill run executes:

  1. Shared URLs are loaded from .distill-shares.json
  2. Full article text is extracted (using trafilatura for web articles, FixTweet API for X/Twitter posts)
  3. Content is auto-tagged and classified
  4. Shared links get priority — they're always covered in the digest, before feed items
  5. Extracted content (title, author, full text) is saved back to the share record
  6. Shares are marked as "used" after being included in a digest

docs.title: In the dashboard
docs.text:
The Shares page (/shares) shows:

  • Pending shares — waiting for the next intake run
  • Processed shares — already included in a digest, with extracted content

Click any card to open the full article reader with formatted markdown content.

docs.title: X/Twitter support
docs.text:
Sharing X/Twitter links works automatically. Distill uses the FixTweet API to extract:

  • Tweet text
  • X Articles (long-form posts)
  • Linked article content (resolves t.co redirects and fetches the real article)

No Twitter API key needed.

docs.title: CLI alternative
docs.text:
You can also share URLs from your terminal:

uv run python -m distill share "https://example.com/article" --note "great read"

List pending shares:

uv run python -m distill shares

docs.title: API reference
docs.text:
The shares API accepts both GET (for iOS Shortcuts) and POST (for programmatic use):

# Save a URL (GET — for iOS Shortcuts)
curl "http://localhost:6107/api/shares?url=https://example.com/article"

# Save a URL (POST — with note)
curl -X POST http://localhost:6107/api/shares \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url": "https://example.com/article", "note": "check this out"}'

# List all shares
curl http://localhost:6107/api/shares

# Delete a share
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:6107/api/shares/<id>

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Shortcut says "Could not connect to server"

  • Make sure Tailscale is connected on both devices
  • Verify the server is running: curl http://<tailscale-ip>:6107/api/shares
  • Try using the numeric IP instead of hostname

URL not appearing in dashboard?

  • Open http://<your-address>:6107/shares and check if it's listed
  • The shortcut uses the clipboard — make sure you copied the URL before running it

Content shows "not fetched"?

  • Content is extracted during the intake pipeline run, not at share time
  • Run uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights to trigger enrichment
  • Some sites block scraping — the article text may not be extractable

docs.title: Daily Automation
docs.text: Run Distill automatically every morning so a fresh digest is waiting when you start your day.

docs.title: macOS (launchd)
docs.text: Distill ships with a launchd plist that runs the full pipeline daily at 6:00 AM.

docs.title: Step 1: Edit the plist
docs.text:
Copy and customize the template:

cp support/com.distill.daily.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/

Open ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.distill.daily.plist and update these paths:

<!-- Update this to your distill project path -->
<string>/path/to/distill</string>

<!-- Update the uv --project path -->
<string>uv</string>
<string>run</string>
<string>--project</string>
<string>/path/to/distill</string>

Find your paths

# Your distill project path
pwd  # run from the distill directory

# Your uv path
which uv

docs.title: Step 2: Set your API key
docs.text:
The launchd environment doesn't inherit your shell's export statements. Add your API key to the plist's EnvironmentVariables section, or use a .env file in the project root:

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-...

docs.title: Step 3: Load the agent
docs.text: launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.distill.daily.plist

docs.title: Step 4: Verify
docs.text:
Check if it's loaded:

launchctl list | grep distill

View logs:

tail -f ~/Library/Logs/distill-daily.log

Trigger a manual run to test:

launchctl start com.distill.daily

docs.title: Unload
docs.text: launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.distill.daily.plist

docs.title: Linux (cron)
docs.text:
crontab -e

Add:

0 6 * * * cd /path/to/distill && ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... /path/to/uv run python -m distill run --output ./insights --use-defaults >> ~/distill.log 2>&1

docs.title: Linux (systemd timer)
docs.text:
Create ~/.config/systemd/user/distill.service:

[Unit]
Description=Distill daily pipeline

[Service]
Type=oneshot
WorkingDirectory=/path/to/distill
Environment=ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
ExecStart=/path/to/uv run python -m distill run --output ./insights --use-defaults

Create ~/.config/systemd/user/distill.timer:

[Unit]
Description=Run Distill daily at 6am

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* 06:00:00
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

Enable:

systemctl --user enable --now distill.timer

docs.title: What distill run does
docs.text:
The run command orchestrates the full pipeline:

  1. Sessions — Parses Claude Code / Codex CLI sessions
  2. Journal — Generates a dev journal entry from sessions
  3. Intake — Fetches RSS feeds, enriches articles, synthesizes digest
  4. Blog — Generates weekly/thematic blog posts from journal entries

You can skip steps:

# Only intake (reading digest), skip sessions/journal/blog
uv run python -m distill run --output ./insights --use-defaults --skip-sessions --skip-blog

docs.title: Web dashboard + automation
docs.text:
If you also want the web dashboard running permanently, use a separate launchd agent or run it in a tmux session:

# In a tmux session
uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights

The dashboard reads from ./insights/ — it picks up new digests automatically when the daily pipeline completes.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Distill is configured via .distill.toml. The file is searched in order:

  1. .distill.toml in the current working directory
  2. ~/.config/distill/.distill.toml

Environment variables and CLI flags override TOML values.

docs.title: Minimal config
docs.text:
[user]
name = "Your Name"
role = "software engineer"

[intake]
use_defaults = true

This uses the built-in 90+ RSS feeds and default settings. Everything else is optional.

docs.title: [user] — Identity
docs.text:
Injected into LLM prompts so Claude tailors the digest to you.

[user]
name = "Jane Doe"
role = "ML engineer"
bio = "Building recommendation systems at Acme Corp"

docs.title: [output]
docs.text:
[output]
directory = "./insights"  # where all output goes

docs.title: [intake] — Reading digest
docs.text:
[intake]
use_defaults = true          # include built-in 90+ feeds
target_word_count = 800      # digest length
model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"  # LLM model
publishers = ["obsidian"]    # output format

# Custom RSS feeds (added on top of defaults)
rss_feeds = [
    "https://blog.example.com/feed",
    "https://newsletter.example.com/rss",
]

# Or point to a file with one URL per line
feeds_file = "my-feeds.txt"

# Or import from your RSS reader
opml_file = "subscriptions.opml"

# Optional sources
browser_history = false
substack_blogs = []

docs.title: [journal] — Dev journal
docs.text:
Generated from Claude Code / Codex CLI sessions.

[journal]
style = "dev-journal"         # dev-journal | tech-blog | team-update | building-in-public
target_word_count = 600
model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
memory_window_days = 7

docs.title: [blog] — Blog posts
docs.text:
Synthesized from journal entries.

[blog]
target_word_count = 1200
include_diagrams = true       # Mermaid diagrams
model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
platforms = ["obsidian"]      # obsidian | ghost | markdown | postiz

docs.title: [sessions]
docs.text:
[sessions]
sources = ["claude", "codex"]
include_global = false        # scan ~/.claude, ~/.codex
since_days = 2

docs.title: [[projects]] — Project context
docs.text:
Tell Claude about your projects so it can reference them intelligently.

[[projects]]
name = "MyApp"
description = "A React Native mobile app for habit tracking"
url = "https://github.com/you/myapp"
tags = ["react-native", "mobile"]

[[projects]]
name = "DataPipe"
description = "ETL pipeline processing 10M events/day on AWS"
tags = ["python", "aws", "data"]

docs.title: [discovery] — Active web search
docs.text:
Searches the web for recent content about your topics. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

[discovery]
topics = ["AI agents", "LLM evaluation", "Claude MCP"]
people = ["Simon Willison", "Hamel Husain"]
max_results_per_query = 5
max_age_days = 3

docs.title: [social] — Brand hashtags
docs.text:
[social]
brand_hashtags = ["#BuildInPublic"]
secondary_hashtags = ["#AI", "#LLM"]

docs.title: [server]
docs.text:
[server]
hostname = "your-machine.ts.net"  # for iOS Shortcut sharing URL

docs.title: [notifications]
docs.text:
[notifications]
enabled = true
slack_webhook = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/..."
ntfy_url = "https://ntfy.sh"
ntfy_topic = "distill"

docs.title: [intelligence]
docs.text:
[intelligence]
model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"  # cheap model for entity extraction

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Any config value can be overridden via environment variable. See the full environment variable reference.

The most important one:

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-...

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get Distill running in 5 minutes. All you need is Python and an Anthropic API key.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Requirement Version Install Python 3.11+ python.org uv latest curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh \| sh Bun latest curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install \| bash (for web dashboard)

docs.title: Step 1: Clone and install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/nikspatel007/distill.git
cd distill
uv sync

This installs all Python dependencies including trafilatura (article extraction), feedparser (RSS), and the anthropic SDK.

docs.title: Step 2: Set your API key
docs.text:
Environment variable.env file 
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-...

Create a .env file in the project root:

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-...

Distill uses the Anthropic Python SDK directly. The default model is claude-sonnet-4-6.

docs.title: Step 3: Run your first digest
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --use-defaults

This will:

  1. Fetch articles from 90+ built-in RSS feeds (tech, AI, engineering)
  2. Extract full text from each article using trafilatura
  3. Auto-tag and classify content
  4. Send everything to Claude for synthesis
  5. Write a markdown digest to ./insights/intake/YYYY-MM-DD.md

First run takes 2-3 minutes

Most time is spent fetching RSS feeds and extracting article text. The LLM synthesis itself takes ~30 seconds.

docs.title: Step 4: Read your digest
docs.text:
Open the generated file:

cat ./insights/intake/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md

Or start the web dashboard:

uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights

Then open http://localhost:6107 in your browser.

docs.title: Step 5: Add your own feeds
docs.text:
Create .distill.toml in the project root:

[user]
name = "Your Name"
role = "software engineer"

[intake]
use_defaults = true  # keep the built-in 90+ feeds
rss_feeds = [
    "https://your-favorite-blog.com/feed",
    "https://another-blog.com/rss.xml",
]

Run again:

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights

docs.title: What's next
docs.text:
• Automate it: Set up a daily cron so digests appear every morning
  • Web dashboard: Start the dashboard to browse digests
  • Share from phone: Set up iOS Shortcut to share articles
  • Customize: Full configuration reference

docs.title: Full pipeline (optional)
docs.text:
If you also use Claude Code or Codex CLI, Distill can generate a dev journal from your coding sessions and synthesize blog posts:

# Everything: sessions + journal + intake + blog
uv run python -m distill run --output ./insights --use-defaults --global

The --global flag scans ~/.claude and ~/.codex for session data.

docs.title: Ghost CMS
docs.text: Publish intake digests and blog posts directly to a Ghost blog.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. In your Ghost admin panel, go to Settings > Integrations > Add custom integration
  2. Name it "Distill" and copy the Admin API Key (format: id:secret)
  3. Note your Ghost URL (e.g., https://your-blog.ghost.io)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
.distill.tomlEnvironment variables 
[ghost]
url = "https://your-blog.ghost.io"
admin_api_key = "abc123:def456..."
newsletter_slug = "your-newsletter"  # optional: auto-send to subscribers
auto_publish = true
blog_as_draft = false

export GHOST_URL=https://your-blog.ghost.io
export GHOST_ADMIN_API_KEY=abc123:def456...
export GHOST_NEWSLETTER_SLUG=your-newsletter

docs.title: Publish intake digest to Ghost
docs.text: uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --publish ghost

docs.title: Publish blog posts to Ghost
docs.text: uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights --publish ghost

docs.title: Multiple Ghost sites
docs.text:
Name your targets in the config:

[ghost]
default = "personal"

[ghost.personal]
url = "https://personal-blog.ghost.io"
admin_api_key = "abc:123..."

[ghost.work]
url = "https://work-blog.ghost.io"
admin_api_key = "def:456..."

Use a specific target:

uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights --publish ghost --target work

docs.title: What gets published
docs.text:
• Blog posts are created as Ghost posts with proper HTML formatting
  • Tags from the digest/post are mapped to Ghost tags
  • Newsletter sending is triggered automatically if newsletter_slug is set
  • Duplicate detection: Ghost integration checks for existing posts by slug

docs.title: Postiz (Social Media)
docs.text: Postiz is a self-hosted social media scheduler. Distill pushes content to Postiz as drafts, which you can review and publish from the Postiz UI.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Deploy Postiz via Docker (see Postiz docs)
  2. Get your API key from the Postiz settings

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
[postiz]
url = "https://your-postiz-instance.com"
api_key = "your-api-key"
default_type = "draft"          # draft | schedule | now

Or via environment variables:

export POSTIZ_URL=https://your-postiz-instance.com
export POSTIZ_API_KEY=your-api-key

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Push blog posts as Postiz drafts
uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights --publish postiz

# Push intake digest
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --publish postiz

docs.title: Scheduling
docs.text:
Postiz can auto-schedule posts:

[postiz]
schedule_enabled = true
timezone = "America/New_York"
weekly_time = "09:00"
weekly_day = 0                  # Monday
daily_social_enabled = true
daily_social_time = "08:00"
daily_social_platforms = ["linkedin", "twitter"]

docs.title: Supported platforms
docs.text: Postiz supports 20+ platforms including Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Reddit, Bluesky, Mastodon, Instagram, Facebook, and more. Connect platforms in the Postiz admin UI.

docs.title: Optional Sources
docs.text: Beyond RSS feeds, Distill can ingest content from several additional sources. Each requires either an API key or a data export.

docs.title: Reddit
docs.text:
Ingest your saved and upvoted posts.

Install: 

uv add 'distill[reddit]'

Configure: 

[reddit]
client_id = "your-client-id"
client_secret = "your-client-secret"
username = "your-reddit-username"

Create a Reddit app at reddit.com/prefs/apps (choose "script" type).

Run: 

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --sources rss,reddit

docs.title: YouTube
docs.text:
Ingest video metadata and transcripts from your liked/saved videos.

Install: 

uv add 'distill[google]'

Configure: 

export YOUTUBE_API_KEY=your-youtube-api-key

Get a key from Google Cloud Console with YouTube Data API v3 enabled.

Run: 

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --sources rss,youtube

docs.title: Gmail (newsletters)
docs.text:
Ingest newsletters from your Gmail using the List-Unsubscribe header to identify newsletters.

Install: 

uv add 'distill[google]'

Setup:

  1. Create OAuth credentials in Google Cloud Console
  2. Enable the Gmail API
  3. Download credentials.json

Run: 

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --gmail-credentials path/to/credentials.json

The first run opens a browser for OAuth consent. A token.json is saved for subsequent runs.

docs.title: Browser history
docs.text:
Read recently visited URLs from Chrome or Safari.

No extra installation needed.

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --browser-history

Common domains (Google, GitHub, localhost, StackOverflow) are filtered out by default. Customize the blocklist in .distill.toml:

[intake]
browser_history = true

docs.title: Substack
docs.text:
Ingest posts from specific Substack newsletters:

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights \
  --substack-blogs "https://newsletter.example.com,https://another.substack.com"

Or in config:

[intake]
substack_blogs = [
    "https://newsletter.example.com",
    "https://another.substack.com",
]

This fetches the Substack RSS feed and extracts full article text.

docs.title: LinkedIn (GDPR export)
docs.text:
Import from your LinkedIn data export:

  1. Request your data at linkedin.com/mypreferences/d/download-my-data
  2. Download and extract the ZIP
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights \
  --linkedin-export path/to/linkedin-export/

Reads: shares, articles, saved items, and reactions.

docs.title: Twitter/X (data export)
docs.text:
Import from your X data export:

  1. Request your data at x.com/settings/download_your_data
  2. Download and extract the ZIP
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights \
  --twitter-export path/to/twitter-export/

docs.title: Combining sources
docs.text:
Use --sources to run specific sources, or omit it to auto-detect all configured sources:

# Specific sources
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --sources rss,reddit,youtube

# All configured sources (auto-detected from config + env)
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights

docs.title: Blog
docs.text: The blog pipeline synthesizes weekly and thematic blog posts from your journal entries and reading digests.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text: uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights

docs.title: Weekly synthesis
docs.text:
Summarizes a week of journal entries into a cohesive blog post.

uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights --type weekly --week 2026-W09

docs.title: Thematic deep-dives
docs.text:
Detects recurring themes across journal entries and generates focused posts.

uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights --type thematic

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
[blog]
target_word_count = 1200
include_diagrams = true        # Mermaid diagrams
model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
platforms = ["obsidian"]       # where to publish

docs.title: Obsidian (default)
docs.text: Blog posts are written to ./insights/blog/ as markdown with YAML frontmatter and Obsidian wiki links.

docs.title: Ghost CMS
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights \
  --publish ghost \
  --ghost-url https://your-blog.ghost.io \
  --ghost-key "id:secret"

See Ghost integration.

docs.title: Multiple platforms
docs.text: uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights --publish obsidian,ghost,postiz

docs.title: Memory
docs.text: The blog pipeline tracks what it has generated (.blog-state.json) to avoid regenerating existing posts. It also maintains cross-post memory (.blog-memory.json) to avoid repeating examples.

docs.title: Editorial notes
docs.text:
Guide the blog pipeline with editorial notes:

uv run python -m distill note "Focus on the multi-agent architecture this week" \
  --target "week:2026-W09"

Notes are injected into the LLM prompt for the specified target.

docs.title: Intake (Reading Digest)
docs.text: The intake pipeline ingests content from RSS feeds and other sources, then uses Claude to synthesize a daily reading digest.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text: uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --use-defaults

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
RSS feeds, shared URLs, browser history, etc.
    ↓
Content parsers (source-specific)
    ↓
Canonical ContentItem list
    ↓
Enrichment: full-text extraction, auto-tagging, entity extraction
    ↓
Clustering by topic
    ↓
Claude synthesis → daily digest markdown
    ↓
Publishers: Obsidian, Ghost, Postiz

docs.title: Built-in RSS feeds (default)
docs.text:
Distill ships with 90+ curated feeds covering AI, engineering, and tech. Enable with --use-defaults or:

[intake]
use_defaults = true

docs.title: Custom RSS feeds
docs.text:
[intake]
rss_feeds = [
    "https://blog.example.com/feed",
    "https://newsletter.example.com/rss.xml",
]

Or use OPML export from your RSS reader:

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --opml subscriptions.opml

docs.title: Shared URLs
docs.text: URLs shared from your phone or CLI get priority treatment in the digest. See Sharing from Phone.

docs.title: Browser history
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --browser-history

Reads Chrome and Safari history. Filters out common domains (Google, GitHub, localhost).

docs.title: Discovery (active search)
docs.text:
Searches the web for topics you care about:

[discovery]
topics = ["AI agents", "LLM evaluation"]
people = ["Simon Willison"]
max_results_per_query = 5
max_age_days = 3

docs.title: Other sources
docs.text: Source Flag / Config Extra deps Substack --substack-blogs URL1,URL2 None Reddit --reddit-user NAME praw YouTube --youtube-api-key KEY google-api-python-client Gmail --gmail-credentials path/to/creds.json google-api-python-client LinkedIn --linkedin-export path/to/export/ None Twitter/X --twitter-export path/to/export/ None Claude sessions --include-sessions --global-sessions None

docs.title: Enrichment
docs.text:
Every content item goes through:

  1. Full-text extraction — trafilatura fetches and parses the full article
  2. Auto-tagging — keyword-based topic detection
  3. Entity extraction — LLM identifies people, companies, technologies
  4. Classification — LLM categorizes content type and relevance

Shared URLs are enriched first with a dedicated budget to guarantee coverage.

docs.title: Deduplication
docs.text:
The intake pipeline tracks processed items in .intake-state.json. Running twice on the same day won't re-process articles. Use --force to bypass:

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --force

docs.title: Dry run
docs.text:
Preview what would be processed without calling the LLM:

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --dry-run

docs.title: Output
docs.text: The digest is written to ./insights/intake/YYYY-MM-DD.md in Obsidian-compatible markdown with YAML frontmatter.

docs.title: Seed ideas
docs.text:
Inject your own topics into the digest:

uv run python -m distill seed "The intersection of AI and climate modeling"

Seeds are consumed by the next intake run and marked as used.

docs.title: Journal
docs.text: The journal pipeline generates daily developer journal entries from your Claude Code and Codex CLI sessions.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill journal --output ./insights --global

The --global flag scans ~/.claude and ~/.codex for session data.

docs.title: What it produces
docs.text: A markdown journal entry for each day you had coding sessions, written by Claude based on what you actually built. Example output in ./insights/journal/2026-03-04.md.

docs.title: Styles
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill journal --output ./insights --style dev-journal
Style Description dev-journal Technical narrative of what you built (default) tech-blog Blog-ready writeup of your work team-update Standup-style summary for team building-in-public Public-facing progress update

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
[journal]
style = "dev-journal"
target_word_count = 600
model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
memory_window_days = 7

docs.title: Memory
docs.text: The journal keeps a rolling memory (.working-memory.json) so entries can reference previous days. This avoids repetition and builds narrative continuity.

docs.title: Specific dates
docs.text:
# Generate for a specific date
uv run python -m distill journal --output ./insights --date 2026-03-01

# Generate for all days since a date
uv run python -m distill journal --output ./insights --since 2026-03-01

docs.title: Project-scoped
docs.text:
uv run python -m distill journal --output ./insights --project MyApp

Only includes sessions related to the specified project.

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text: All commands are run with uv run python -m distill <command> or the distill script if installed.

docs.title: distill (bare)
docs.text:
Run the full pipeline and start the web server.

uv run python -m distill --output ./insights
Flag Default Description --dir, -d . Session directory --output, -o ./insights Output directory --port, -p 6107 Web server port --no-run Skip pipeline, just start server --no-serve Skip server, just run pipeline --verbose, -v Verbose logging --version, -V Show version

docs.title: distill run
docs.text:
Full pipeline: sessions + journal + intake + blog.

uv run python -m distill run --output ./insights --use-defaults
Flag Default Description --output, -o ./insights Output directory --dir, -d . Session directory --global false Scan ~/.claude, ~/.codex --use-defaults true Use built-in 90+ RSS feeds --publish obsidian Comma-separated publishers --skip-sessions Skip session parsing --skip-intake Skip intake pipeline --skip-blog Skip blog generation --model Override LLM model --force Bypass deduplication --dry-run Preview without LLM calls --since 2 days ago Date to look back from

docs.title: distill intake
docs.text:
Ingest feeds and generate a reading digest.

uv run python -m distill intake --output ./insights --use-defaults
Flag Default Description --output, -o required Output directory --sources, -s auto-detect Comma-separated: rss,reddit,youtube,... --use-defaults Use built-in feeds --feeds-file Path to feeds list file --opml Path to OPML file --model Override LLM model --words 800 Target word count --publish obsidian Output publishers --force Reprocess all items --dry-run Preview context --browser-history Include browser history --substack-blogs Comma-separated Substack URLs --reddit-user Reddit username --youtube-api-key YouTube API key --gmail-credentials Path to Gmail credentials.json --include-sessions Include coding sessions --global-sessions Scan global session dirs

docs.title: distill journal
docs.text:
Generate journal entries from coding sessions.

uv run python -m distill journal --output ./insights --global
Flag Default Description --output, -o ./insights Output directory --dir, -d . Session directory --style, -s dev-journal Style preset --date today Specific date --since Generate since date --global false Scan global sessions --words 600 Target word count --model Override model --force Regenerate cached --dry-run Preview context

docs.title: distill blog
docs.text:
Generate blog posts from journal entries.

uv run python -m distill blog --output ./insights
Flag Default Description --output, -o required Output directory --type, -t all weekly, thematic, or all --week Specific week: 2026-W09 --publish Publishers: obsidian,ghost,postiz --model Override model --words 1200 Target word count --force Regenerate existing --dry-run Preview context

docs.title: distill serve
docs.text:
Start the web dashboard.

uv run python -m distill serve --output ./insights
Flag Default Description --output, -o ./insights Output directory --port, -p 6107 HTTP port --dev Dev mode (API only, no static) --tls-cert TLS certificate path --tls-key TLS private key path

docs.title: distill share
docs.text:
Share a URL for the next intake digest.

uv run python -m distill share "https://example.com" --note "interesting"

docs.title: distill shares
docs.text:
List shared URLs.

uv run python -m distill shares --all

docs.title: distill seed
docs.text:
Add a topic idea for the next digest.

uv run python -m distill seed "AI agents for code review" --tags "ai,agents"

docs.title: distill seeds
docs.text:
List seed ideas.

uv run python -m distill seeds --all

docs.title: distill note
docs.text:
Add an editorial note to guide content.

uv run python -m distill note "Focus on MCP servers" --target "week:2026-W09"

docs.title: distill status
docs.text:
Show pipeline state and statistics.

uv run python -m distill status --output ./insights

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: All environment variables Distill reads. Only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is required.

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Variable Description ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Claude API key for LLM synthesis

docs.title: LLM
docs.text: Variable Default Description DISTILL_MODEL claude-sonnet-4-6 Override model for all pipelines DISTILL_USE_CLI Set to 1 to force claude -p subprocess

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Variable Default Description DISTILL_OUTPUT_DIR ./insights Override output directory

docs.title: Ghost CMS
docs.text:
Variable Description GHOST_URL Ghost instance URL GHOST_ADMIN_API_KEY Admin API key (id:secret format) GHOST_NEWSLETTER_SLUG Newsletter for auto-send

For named targets (e.g., personal, work):

Variable Description GHOST_PERSONAL_URL Named target URL GHOST_PERSONAL_ADMIN_API_KEY Named target key

docs.title: Postiz
docs.text: Variable Default Description POSTIZ_URL Postiz instance URL POSTIZ_API_KEY API key POSTIZ_DEFAULT_TYPE draft draft, schedule, or now POSTIZ_SCHEDULE_ENABLED false Enable scheduling POSTIZ_TIMEZONE America/Chicago Scheduling timezone

docs.title: Reddit
docs.text: Variable Description REDDIT_CLIENT_ID Reddit OAuth app client ID REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET Reddit OAuth app client secret REDDIT_USERNAME Reddit username REDDIT_PASSWORD Reddit password

docs.title: YouTube
docs.text: Variable Description YOUTUBE_API_KEY YouTube Data API v3 key

docs.title: Image generation
docs.text: Variable Description GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY Google Gemini API key

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text: Variable Description DISTILL_SLACK_WEBHOOK Slack incoming webhook URL DISTILL_NTFY_URL ntfy.sh base URL DISTILL_NTFY_TOPIC ntfy topic (default: distill)

docs.title: TLS / Server
docs.text: Variable Default Description TLS_CERT Path to TLS certificate TLS_KEY Path to TLS private key TLS_PORT 6117 HTTPS port

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
After running the pipeline, your output directory (default ./insights) contains:

insights/
├── intake/                          # Daily reading digests
│   └── 2026-03-04.md
│
├── journal/                         # Dev journal entries
│   └── 2026-03-04.md
│
├── blog/                            # Blog posts
│   ├── weekly-2026-W09.md
│   └── multi-agent-patterns.md      # Thematic posts
│
├── sessions/                        # Per-session Obsidian notes
│   └── session-note.md
│
├── daily/                           # Daily session summaries
│   └── daily-2026-03-04.md
│
├── weekly/                          # Weekly session digests
│   └── week-2026-W09.md
│
├── projects/                        # Per-project notes
│   └── my-project.md
│
├── brainstorm/                      # Content ideas
│   └── 2026-03-04.json
│
├── index.md                         # Session index
│
# State files (hidden, managed automatically):
├── .distill-memory.json             # Unified cross-pipeline memory
├── .distill-seeds.json              # Seed ideas
├── .distill-shares.json             # Shared URLs
├── .distill-notes.json              # Editorial notes
├── .distill-content-store.json      # Vector store (JSON fallback)
├── .distill-graph.json              # Knowledge graph
├── .intake-state.json               # Intake deduplication
├── .distill-last-run.json           # Last pipeline run report
├── .daily-social-state.json         # Social post state
├── .working-memory.json             # Journal memory
├── .intake-memory.json              # Intake memory
├── .blog-state.json                 # Blog generation state
└── .blog-memory.json                # Blog cross-post memory

docs.title: Obsidian integration
docs.text:
The default publisher uses Obsidian-compatible markdown:

  • YAML frontmatter with tags, dates, and metadata
  • [[Wiki links]] for cross-referencing
  • Mermaid code blocks for diagrams

Drop the insights/ folder into an Obsidian vault and everything links together.

docs.title: State files
docs.text:
State files (prefixed with .) are managed automatically. They track:

  • Which articles have been processed (deduplication)
  • Cross-session memory (themes, entities, trends)
  • Blog generation state (which posts exist)
  • Seeds and shares (pending vs used)

You can safely delete any state file to reset that subsystem. The pipeline will rebuild from scratch on the next run.
